signals.reasoning: BTC is still the cleanest long on the board after the ETH stop, with negative funding, trade-grade mechanical score, and price/OI continuing to read trend-supported. It held up well enough to keep the earlier conditional long thesis alive on a CLEAR board.

ETH still has negative funding support, but the price/OI pattern remains short-covering-rally rather than clean continuation and crowding is elevated. Better to stand down after the stop rather than re-force the same idea immediately.
SOL remains constructive and trend-supported, but its funding edge is weaker and the setup is still less compelling than BTC for a fresh long. Watch-only.
AZTEC is improving, but the setup still lacks enough reliable structure and remains too execution-sensitive to trust as a fresh signal. Hold only.
PEOPLE is stabilizing but still looks more like recovery noise than a clean fresh trend. Not strong enough to upgrade.
MON has mildly constructive tape, but positive funding and a less differentiated setup keep it below the bar for a real long recommendation. Watch-only.
PROMPT is still too thin and OI-heavy to trust despite the bounce. It does not offer a better edge than the stronger major setup in BTC.

PURR remains the clearest bearish lean on the sheet with a negative mechanical score and weak price behavior versus the rest of the board. It is still a thinner alt, so confidence stays moderate, but this is the best short candidate here.

STBL is heavily crowded on the long side with very positive funding and high OI-to-volume, making it too dangerous to chase despite the trend-supported tape. Also too awkward to short cleanly while momentum is still holding up.
TST is still too extended with positive funding and elevated crowding after the squeeze-style run. Too late to chase, not clean enough to short.
MERL still has constructive recovery structure, but positive funding and high cascade risk make it a weaker follow-through long than BTC. Keep it on the watchlist.
